scenario_text: An employee raised a misconduct concern through a speak-up channel, but managers then excluded the reporter from key meetings while the case team skipped parts of the required investigation protocol. The immediate issue is not whether the allegation itself is true yet, but whether retaliation protections and the compliance response process are being followed.

scenario_context.operating_context: Internal speak-up and case-management process for control-sensitive conduct concerns

event_path_summary: A speak-up concern is mishandled because retaliation protections and investigation steps are not being followed.
primary_driver: Breakdown in whistleblowing, non-retaliation, and investigation process discipline
main_business_consequence: The organisation faces control and credibility pressure because the speak-up process may no longer be trusted or defensible.

valid_risks.title: Whistleblowing and non-retaliation process failure
valid_risks.why_valid: The event is about whether the speak-up process itself is being handled correctly.

valid_risks.title: Investigation-governance breakdown
valid_risks.why_valid: Required investigation steps were skipped in responding to the concern.

valid_risks.title: Code-of-conduct enforcement credibility risk
valid_risks.why_valid: Trust in the compliance response process is weakened if retaliation is tolerated.

invalid_risks.title: Malicious insider cyber misuse
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No privileged-system abuse or cyber event is the primary trigger.

invalid_risks.title: Routine HR grievance only
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The issue is broader than ordinary employee relations because formal speak-up protections apply.

invalid_risks.title: Regulatory filing failure
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No filing or reporting deadline is the core event.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The initiating failure is mishandling of a speak-up and investigation process, which is a Compliance event.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Fraud / Integrity may become relevant if the allegation is substantiated, but the current event is the response-process failure.
People / Workforce is adjacent because retaliation affects an employee, yet the controlled compliance process is the main issue.
Cyber is not relevant because there is no system misuse trigger.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Treating retaliation and investigation wording as generic HR or cyber insider language instead of compliance-process failure.

scenario_text: A commercial team arranged sponsored travel, accommodation, and hospitality for a public official through an intermediary while approval records were incomplete and the business rationale remained vague. No cash transfer is yet proven improper, but the value-transfer pattern creates anti-bribery exposure that is more specific than a generic policy lapse.

scenario_context.operating_context: Intermediary-managed relationship and business-development activity involving a public official

event_path_summary: A public-official hospitality arrangement creates bribery exposure because value transfer and approvals are weakly controlled.
primary_driver: Potentially improper value transfer through hospitality and intermediary use
main_business_consequence: The organisation faces integrity, enforcement, and remediation pressure because anti-bribery controls may have been bypassed.

valid_risks.title: Bribery and corruption exposure
valid_risks.why_valid: The event path is explicit anti-bribery risk around value transfer to a public official.

valid_risks.title: Improper hospitality and sponsored-travel risk
valid_risks.why_valid: Hospitality and travel are the mechanisms creating the exposure.

valid_risks.title: Intermediary oversight weakness in public-official engagement
valid_risks.why_valid: The intermediary structure weakens confidence in how the interaction was controlled.

invalid_risks.title: Generic policy breach only
invalid_risks.why_invalid: There is a more specific integrity risk than a general compliance-process lapse.

invalid_risks.title: Supplier delivery issue
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No physical supply-chain event drives the scenario.

invalid_risks.title: Cyber compromise
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No technology event initiates the exposure.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The event is specifically about bribery-style exposure in a public-official interaction, which makes Fraud / Integrity primary.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Compliance is adjacent because policies apply, but the value-transfer risk is the main event path.
Third-Party is secondary because an intermediary is involved, yet the issue is not generic partner governance alone.
Regulatory attention may follow, but it is downstream of the integrity event.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Flattening public-official hospitality scenarios into generic compliance instead of bribery-corruption exposure.

scenario_text: A remote engineer accessed export-controlled systems from a restricted jurisdiction while the required screening and trade-control clearance were still incomplete. The problem is not a general geopolitical backdrop alone; it is that an explicit sanctions and export-control restriction may have been bypassed before the work was allowed to proceed.

scenario_context.operating_context: Remote technical support and access governance for controlled systems

event_path_summary: Controlled-system access proceeded despite trade-control restrictions tied to a restricted jurisdiction.
primary_driver: Sanctions and export-control screening failure before regulated access was allowed
main_business_consequence: The organisation faces regulatory and legal exposure because restricted-jurisdiction access may have violated trade controls.

valid_risks.title: Sanctions and export-control breach exposure
valid_risks.why_valid: The core event is possible bypass of trade-control restrictions.

valid_risks.title: Restricted-jurisdiction access-control failure
valid_risks.why_valid: Access was enabled before the required trade-control clearance was complete.

valid_risks.title: Trade-screening governance weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: The access decision outpaced screening and regulatory checks.

invalid_risks.title: Routine policy attestation housekeeping
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The scenario is more specific than an ordinary internal policy task because trade-control restrictions are explicit.

invalid_risks.title: Privilege-abuse cyber intrusion
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No malicious system abuse is described as the primary event.

invalid_risks.title: Travel itinerary disruption
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No continuity or logistics issue is central.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The primary event is a possible sanctions or export-control failure, which makes Regulatory the right lens.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Compliance is adjacent because process discipline failed, but the trade-control restriction is the defining feature.
Geopolitical context matters, yet the event is not just market-access concern in the abstract.
Cyber is not primary because the problem is not hostile system misuse.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Treating restricted-jurisdiction export-control wording as generic compliance or cyber access instead of a regulatory trade-controls event.

scenario_text: A new business partner was approved through escalation even though beneficial-ownership checks remained incomplete and several due-diligence red flags were still unresolved. There is no prohibited-party match yet and no proven bribery event, but the inherited third-party control posture is materially weaker than the standard approval path requires.

scenario_context.operating_context: Business partner onboarding and due-diligence approval for a commercially important relationship

event_path_summary: A partner was onboarded despite unresolved due-diligence red flags, weakening inherited third-party control assurance.
primary_driver: Weak third-party due diligence and escalation discipline
main_business_consequence: The organisation carries inherited third-party risk because partner approval outpaced evidence that control expectations were met.

valid_risks.title: Business-partner control weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: The main issue is weak inherited assurance over the partner relationship.

valid_risks.title: Due-diligence escalation weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: Escalation allowed onboarding before red flags were resolved.

valid_risks.title: Third-party monitoring and onboarding gap
valid_risks.why_valid: The partner entered the environment without full evidence that control expectations were met.

invalid_risks.title: Confirmed sanctions breach
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No prohibited-party match or explicit trade-control violation is yet stated.

invalid_risks.title: Bribery event as primary
invalid_risks.why_invalid: Integrity exposure may be adjacent, but no corrupt payment or public-official interaction is described.

invalid_risks.title: Supplier delivery delay
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No physical supply or logistics issue drives the scenario.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The event path is inherited control weakness from a partner-approved despite unresolved diligence concerns, which fits Third-Party.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Compliance is adjacent because process obligations apply, but the relationship-governance weakness is the main issue.
Fraud / Integrity is nearby because beneficial ownership and red flags matter, yet there is no explicit bribery or deception trigger.
Regulatory risk is secondary until an explicit prohibited-party or sanctions fact appears.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Promoting every diligence-red-flag scenario into generic compliance instead of third-party control weakness.

scenario_text: A restricted employee discussed material non-public information during a blackout period before the disclosure-control process was complete, and monitoring later found that the handling standard for inside information had not been followed consistently. The event is not a malicious cyber insider scenario; it is a market-conduct and policy-governance lapse around sensitive information handling.

scenario_context.operating_context: Handling of inside information and disclosure-control obligations around sensitive announcements

event_path_summary: Inside-information handling and blackout restrictions were not followed before disclosure controls were complete.
primary_driver: Policy and disclosure-control failure over material non-public information
main_business_consequence: The organisation faces conduct and remediation pressure because sensitive information handling may not have met required restrictions.

valid_risks.title: Insider-information handling failure
valid_risks.why_valid: The event explicitly concerns restricted treatment of material non-public information.

valid_risks.title: Disclosure-control process weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: The controlled sequence for handling and disclosure was not followed correctly.

valid_risks.title: Market-conduct policy breach
valid_risks.why_valid: Blackout and sensitive-information rules were not applied consistently.

invalid_risks.title: Duplicate payment release fraud
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The issue is not a disbursement or payment-control event.

invalid_risks.title: Warehouse aisle congestion
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No warehouse throughput or logistics-queue problem is involved.

invalid_risks.title: Supplier governance issue
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No third-party relationship defines the event path.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The scenario is primarily about policy and disclosure-control failure around inside information, which is a Compliance event.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Regulatory implications may follow, but the immediate issue is the internal conduct-control failure.
Fraud / Integrity is adjacent because conduct matters, yet no bribery or deceptive payment event appears.
Cyber should not become primary just because the wording includes insider language.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Collapsing insider-information wording into cyber insider misuse because of the word insider.
